channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Here's what to know about the US push for stability in post-Assad Syria
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken has wrapped up perhaps his last Middle East visit as America’s top diplomat, with the aim of preventing Syria from spiraling out of control after the sudden ouster of President Bashar Assad. Blinken was one of several senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Woman arrested for attempting to climb fence outside White House
item.description: A woman was taken into custody by the U.S. Secret Service and arrested on Saturday after she attempted to breach a fence installed outside of the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eyewitness accounts of New Jersey drones include politicians, journalist
item.description: Here are some of the firsthand accounts of the drone sightings, as told to NewsNation by eyewitnesses.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: San Francisco cafe employee takes down would-be thief
item.description: The video (above) shows a man wearing a black hoodie and mask walking into the cafe and trying to take an item from a customer dining inside.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jamie Foxx reportedly injured in altercation at Beverly Hills restaurant
item.description: Foxx, who turned 57 on Friday, was dining with family at Mr. Chow when patrons at a nearby table hurled rude and vulgar insults to the "In Living Color" and "Ray" star, a witness told TMZ.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 6 ways to avoid a financial holiday hangover
item.description: The festivity of December is replaced all too quickly by the due dates of January, when the bills from holiday spending and travel arrive.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, ABC 4

item.title: Thousands of Santas and a few Grinches hit the streets for annual SantaCon bar crawls
item.description: Thousands of Kris Kringles and jolly Old St. Nicks are jamming streets, sidewalks and bars from Adelaide to Vancouver on Saturday for annual SantaCon charity pub crawls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pelosi 'on the mend' after hip replacement surgery in Germany
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.), who was hospitalized during a trip to Luxembourg, underwent hip surgery early Saturday, according to a spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Filipino woman on Indonesia death row recalls a stunning last minute reprieve and 'miracle' transfer
item.description: YOGYAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Filipino death row inmate Mary Jane Fiesta Veloso knelt to pray when officers came to take her to an execution site in May 2015, just a few feet away from her isolation cell on an Indonesian prison island, where a 13-member firing squad was waiting.
item.dc:creator: SLAMET RIYADI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Korea’s parliament votes to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his martial law order
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his stunning and short-lived martial law decree, a move that ended days of political paralysis but set up an intense debate over Yoon's fate, as jubilant crowds roared to celebrate another defiant m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Storms encase Iowa and eastern Nebraska in ice and generate first tornado warning in San Francisco
item.description: A major ice storm has created treacherous driving conditions across Iowa and eastern Nebraska and prompted temporary closures of Interstate 80 after numerous cars and trucks slid off the road.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Proposal to list border-crossing monarch butterflies as 'threatened' draws opposition
item.description: Monarch butterflies could be elevated to "threatened" species as proposed this week by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service, but some officials in Texas say that would interfere with ranches and businesses and hurt the economy.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Drones, planes or UFOs? Americans abuzz over mysterious New Jersey sightings
item.description: CHATHAM, N.J. (AP) — That buzzing coming out of New Jersey? It's unclear if it's drones or something else, but for sure the nighttime sightings are producing tons of talk, a raft of conspiracy theories and craned necks looking skyward.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lawmakers tell Apple, Google to prepare for TikTok ban
item.description: TikTok now plans to appeal to the Supreme Court and has asked the D.C. Circuit to put the law on hold while it appeals the case.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Manchin: Biden 'went completely insane on me' over American Rescue Plan opposition
item.description: “I was going to kill the whole bill. Then the president went completely insane on me,” Manchin said in an interview with news outlet Semafor that was published on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: California DMV apologizes for Cybertruck's offensive license plate
item.description: On Thursday, the license plate in question, which was on a Tesla Cybertruck, was spotlighted in a post to X by StopAntisemitism, a group “dedicated to fighting anti-Semitism.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Krispy Kreme offering green doughnuts for 2 days only
item.description: The chain also has a limited time “Merry Grinchmas Doughnut Collection” to celebrate the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court will take up a challenge related to California's tough vehicle emissions standards
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday said it would take up a business-backed appeal that could make it easier to challenge federal regulations, acting in a dispute related to California's nation-leading standards for vehicle emissions.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court will hear Catholic charitable group's plea to be free from Wisconsin unemployment tax
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday said it would take up a new religious rights case over whether a Catholic charitable organization must pay Wisconsin's employment tax.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO shooter manhunt aided by San Francisco police tip
item.description: Days before Luigi Mangione was found after an officer in the Special Victims Unit of the San Francisco PD recognized Mangione in photos.
item.dc:creator: Laura Ingle, NewsNation

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect Luigi Mangione's grandma left millions to family — excluding felons
item.description: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect Luigi Mangione's grandmother left a fortune to her children and grandchildren so as long as they were not charged or convicted of felonies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey governor wants more federal resources for probe into drone sightings
item.description: TOMS RIVER, N.J. (AP) — Gov. Phil Murphy has asked the Biden administration to put more resources into an investigation of mysterious drone sightings that have been reported in New Jersey and nearby states.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A man has pleaded guilty to the death of a 5-year-old girl living in their Kansas homeless camp
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — A man who lived in a makeshift camp with a homeless 5-year-old Kansas girl and her father pleaded guilty Friday to first-degree murder and rape in connection with the child’s death.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Two states sue Glock over add-on allowing machine gun-like firing
item.description: Officials in New Jersey and Minnesota filed a lawsuit against gun manufacturer Glock Thursday, demanding the company suspend its sale of weapons that can be easily converted with a switch to allow over 1,000 rounds of rapid fire.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Iran has new drone ship in Persian Gulf
item.description: Iran has a new ship with the capability of launching drones, according to satellite images reviewed by Maxar Technologies that were shared with The Hill. The new drone ship, called Shahid Bagheri, is anchored offshore of the Iranian naval port of Bandar Abbas in the Persian Gulf, according to Maxar.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation

item.title: Conan O'Brien's parents die within days of each other
item.description: Dr. Thomas O’Brien and Ruth R. O’Brien, the parents of late-night icon Conan O’Brien, both passed away within days of each other at their home in Brookline, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: OpenAI's Altman will donate $1 million to Trump's inaugural fund
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is planning to make a $1 million personal donation to President-Elect Donald Trump's inauguration fund, joining a number of tech companies and executives who are working to improve their relationships with the incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nephew's Christmas revelation leads to cold-case killer's prison sentence
item.description: A convicted killer who had escaped justice for more than a decade learned Thursday that he will likely spend the rest of his life in prison.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Doctor loses license after patient dies from 'Brazilian Butt Lift': Report
item.description: A Florida doctor has lost his medical license after a patient died from a "Brazilian butt lift," NBC affiliate WESH reports.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, NewsNation

item.title: Duke lacrosse players' rape accuser admits she lied
item.description: The former exotic dancer confessed to lying about the encounter in 2006 during her appearance on the “Let’s Talk with Kat” podcast.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Flight from LaGuardia diverts to JFK after bird strike: American Airlines
item.description: A flight from LaGuardia Airport to North Carolina was diverted to John F. Kennedy International Airport on Thursday evening due to a reported bird strike, according to American Airlines.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nearly half of US teens are online 'constantly': Pew report
item.description: Nearly half of American teenagers say they are online “constantly” despite concerns about the effects of social media and smartphones on their mental health, according to a new report published Thursday by the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Drone mystery: New Jersey homeowners threaten to take matters into their own hands if government doesn't act
item.description: New Jersey residents frustrated by dozens of drone sightings are threatening to take action on their own if the government doesn't start providing answers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law met with Ratcliffe about CIA job: Washington Post
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s daughter-in-law took a private meeting with John Ratcliffe, President-elect Trump’s pick to run the CIA, in an apparent bid for a high-ranking position within the agency, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5

item.title: Luxury real estate broker and his twin held without bail on sex trafficking charges
item.description: A prominent luxury real estate broker and his identical twin brother appeared in a Florida courtroom Thursday on sexual assault charges. Another brother, who co-founded the real estate firm, is also charged in what federal officials call a longtime sex trafficking scheme.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione was reported missing 2 weeks before CEO slain: police
item.description: The parents of Luigi Mangione, the man suspected of shooting and killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, told law enforcement that they hadn't heard from their son in months.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Nicole Johnson, WGN 9
